column1: # kapi_abi.tsv - single source of truth for the kernel symbols gh_hugepage_reserve
# resolves at runtime and the ABI-compatible signatures it expects per version.
# Consumed by:
#   abi/gen_kapi.awk  -> abi/kapi_abi.gen.h   (KAPI_SYMBOLS x-macro compiled into
#                                              the .ko; expected table for helper)
#   abi/kapi_check.c  (boot-time BTF preflight: compares these vs the running
#                      kernel's /sys/kernel/btf/vmlinux, emits disable_kapi list)
# Scope: only Gunyah-capable GKI bases matter -> 6.1 / 6.6 / 6.12 / 6.18. The
# 5.10/5.15 .ko builds are Magisk-template placeholders (no Gunyah), not covered.
# Types are DESUGARED canonical (kCFI compares canonical types; typedefs and
# __bitwise are transparent): gfp_t -> uint, acr_flags_t -> uint. So a row that
# matches by these tokens is guaranteed not to kCFI-trap; a semantic value change
# under an unchanged canonical type (e.g. 6.18 migratetype->acr_flags) is NOT
# visible here and is handled by the module's CONTIG_RANGE version wrapper.
# kind:
#   func  - resolved by kprobe/kallsyms, called through a kapi function pointer.
#   hook  - Android vendor tracepoint; the kernel calls our probe back. The
#           helper WOULD look it up as the BTF typedef btf_trace_<symbol> (its
#           FUNC_PROTO has an EXTRA leading "void *data"; 'params' below omits it)
#           - but GKI vmlinux BTF ships NO btf_trace_* typedefs, so on-device the
#           hook is NOT BTF-checkable and reads "not BTF-checkable" (never
#           disabled). Row kept for documentation + the manual disable_kapi path;
#           the module registers this hook by name at runtime regardless. Its
#           TP_PROTO is identical across 6.1-6.18 (audited), so drift risk is nil.
# Version range is [since, until):  '*' since = from 6.1;  '*' until = open-ended.
# Columns (TAB-separated): symbol  since  until  kind  ret  params  feature
symbol
alloc_contig_pages
alloc_contig_range
prep_compound_page
folio_isolate_lru
folio_isolate_lru
reclaim_pages
reclaim_pages
reclaim_pages
lru_add_drain_all
drop_slab
drain_all_pages
# cma (pool_want_with_cma reservoir): until 6.16 only - the 6.16 migratetype rework
# (MIGRATE_ISOLATE standalone bit) changes pageblock-flag semantics and 6.18 makes
# the setter static; the module compile-gates the feature off at >=6.16 (v9 path),
# so a >=6.16 kernel needs no row (preflight skips, module never resolves).
set_pageblock_migratetype
get_pfnblock_flags_mask
mem_cgroup_from_task
try_to_free_mem_cgroup_pages
try_to_free_mem_cgroup_pages
android_vh_free_one_page_bypass
# moveable_to_cma levers. static_key_enable/disable + kallsyms_lookup_name back
# the restrict_cma_redirect flip (stable signatures, all versions). The two gfp
# adjust hooks are ABI-era exclusive: android_vh_alloc_flags_cma_adjust on
# [6.1,6.12) (called after the built-in ALLOC_CMA decision), replaced by
# android_vh_calc_alloc_flags on [6.12,*) (3-arg, called before). At most one
# exists per kernel; the module registers whichever it finds by name. Like every
# hook they are not BTF-checkable (no btf_trace typedef) so never auto-disabled.
# NOTE: the restrict_cma_redirect DATA symbol itself is intentionally absent -
# it has no call signature / btf_trace typedef, so a row would make the preflight
# hunt a FUNC that isn't there and falsely disable the lever; it is resolved by
# kallsyms_lookup_name at runtime instead.
static_key_enable
static_key_disable
kallsyms_lookup_name
android_vh_alloc_flags_cma_adjust
android_vh_calc_alloc_flags
